content.problemStatement:
Ada has the biggest chessboard in ChefLand. It has NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM), where MвүҘ2NM \ge 2NMвүҘ2N. As training for her upcoming world chess championship match, she placed one bishop at row rrr and column ccc of her board. The remaining squares of the board are empty. Now, she is calculating the number of squares that can be reached by the bishop in at most KKK moves. Help Ada verify her answers.

Remember that in one move, a bishop at row rrr and column ccc can move to another square at row rвҖІr'rвҖІ and column cвҖІc'cвҖІ if and only if either r+c=rвҖІ+cвҖІr+c=r'+c'r+c=rвҖІ+cвҖІ or rвҲ’c=rвҖІвҲ’cвҖІr-c=r'-c'rвҲ’c=rвҖІвҲ’cвҖІ.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains five space-separated integers NNN, MMM, rrr, ccc and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer вҖ” the number of reachable squares.
